channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California's burn scars brace for flooding as freezing rain and snow head for Oregon
item.description: Officials in California distributed sandbags, prepositioned rescue swimmers and told residents to have their go-bags ready as the state prepares for an atmospheric river that could bring heavy rain, winds and flooding to areas recently ravaged by wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elephants trumpet, squeak and flap their ears after their complex move across an Australian city
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Elephants trumpeted, touched trunks and flapped their oversized ears upon reuniting with their herd after a complex, five-day move from an urban Australian zoo to a much larger enclosure.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's pick for education chief seeks approval to lead an agency she would be asked to tear down
item.description: Linda McMahon faces an unusual test as she seeks Senate approval to lead an agency the president wants her to destroy.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Annie Ma, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, Fox 5

item.title: Pilot killed in collision involving Mötley Crüe singer's plane identified
item.description: Joie Vitosky, 78, was certified as a flight engineer since 1978 and a pilot since 2019, according to FAA records.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, NewsNation

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: The White House says news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico are telling “lies.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former longtime Illinois legislative leader Michael Madigan is convicted in corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A Chicago Democrat who once set much of Illinois’ political agenda as the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history was convicted of conspiracy, bribery and wire fraud Wednesday in a split verdict in his high-profile corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California congressman presents ‘D—- Pic’ during subcommittee hearing
item.description: U.S. Rep. Robert Garcia of Long Beach took aim at billionaire Elon Musk’s role as head of the new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) during a subcommittee hearing Wednesday morning. Garcia, a Democrat, referenced committee chair Rep.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: The relationship between the White House and its press corps is time-tested
item.description: This week, the White House barred Associated Press journalists from three media appearances by President Donald Trump — two of them in the Oval Office itself.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to advance Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans on Wednesday released a blueprint they intend to use to advance President Trump’s legislative agenda, breaking through an impasse that had stymied the conference for days.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy fighter jet crashes into San Diego Bay near naval air station; 2 pilots rescued
item.description: Two military pilots were rescued after their Boeing EA-18G Growler jet crashed into the water in San Diego Bay on Wednesday morning. It's unclear what caused the Navy aircraft to crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. Navy fighter jet crashes near Shelter Island while attempting to land
item.description: A military plane crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island while attempting to land, according to the U.S. Navy.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASA, SpaceX reveal new date to return stranded Starliner crew back to Earth
item.description: NASA and SpaceX hope to bring astronauts Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams, who have been stuck on the International Space Station for more than seven months, home in March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: Italy prepares scenic, historic Olympic venues with games now one year away
item.description: This marks the first time Milan will host the Winter Olympics, with the opening ceremony scheduled at San Siro Stadium. The games will feature events in multiple locations, including hockey in a new arena in Milan and various alpine events in Cortina.
item.dc:creator: Matt Barnes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Felony charges filed against man accused of killing pregnant woman in Portage Park
item.description: A man accused of fatally shooting a pregnant woman whose premature child later died is now facing felony charges.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense sounds alarm on unidentified blood at student murders home
item.description: University of Idaho student murder suspect Bryan Kohberger's defense is arguing that two blood samples from unidentified males at the crime scene could clear him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US defense chief Hegseth calls NATO membership for Ukraine unrealistic
item.description: Hegseth made the comments during the first trip to NATO and the Ukraine Defense Contact Group by a member of the new Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inflation ticks up to 3 percent annual increase in January
item.description: The consumer price index (CPI) increased by 0.5 percent in January to hit an annual increase of 3.0 percent after advancing by 2.9 percent in December.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne's 'final show' with original Black Sabbath: What we know
item.description: The performance, called "The Back to the Beginning," will cap off an all-day event featuring sets from several iconic heavy metal acts.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over immigrant deportations and appears to aim directly at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the possibility of Trump serving a third term
item.description: Murmurs from President Trump and his allies have stirred up questions of the incumbent possibly seeking a third term in office, even as he’s currently constitutionally barred from doing so.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9
